spec_match.rationale: The 'list-style-image' option in the dropdown actually generates CSS utilizing 'background-image' on a pseudo-element rather than the 'list-style-image' property, which is misleading.

interactive.rationale: Excellent studio UI with a color picker, custom text input (supporting oklch/rgb), quick palette swatches, property targets, and size sliders.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent blurb aligns with the authoring studio concept.

edge_cases.rationale: While it supports multiple color formats in the text input, the implementation mismatch for list-style-image is an oversight.

references.rationale: The page completely lacks the mandatory ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to design system guidelines using appropriate border and background styles.

openQuestions.title: Fix list-style-image demonstration
openQuestions.detail: In the target property dropdown, the option `list-style-image` actually generates CSS that uses `background-image` on a `::before` pseudo-element instead of the `list-style-image` property. This is misleading. The demo should be updated to either use the actual `list-style-image: image(...)` property (as successfully demonstrated in `color-swatch-lists`), or the option label and CSS should be corrected to 'Custom bullet (background-image)'.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: The page is missing the required reference link to `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5121011285622784`. It should be added to a references or 'see also' section to conform to the repository rules.

summary: A highly functional authoring playground that lets developers test image() with various color syntaxes and properties. However, it contains a misleading implementation for list-style-image (which actually uses background-image on a pseudo-element) and lacks the mandatory ChromeStatus link.
